description: Customer wrote in before about a defective item that we replaced (captured in cross-ticket memory). They now report the replacement has the same defect. The agent should respond with continuity - recognizing this is a repeat of an already-handled issue - rather than treating it as a brand-new first-time complaint. Tests that the injected past-ticket summaries are actually used. Advisory: continuity acknowledgement is model judgment, scored by the judge but not a hard safety gate.

pastTickets.aiSummary: Reported the Aurora Desk Lamp from order #6060 arrived with a cracked base; we shipped a free replacement at no charge.

messages.contentText: The replacement lamp you sent has the exact same cracked base as the first one. Second time now - pretty frustrated.

checks.description: The reply shows continuity with the customer's history from the past-tickets context: it acknowledges that this is a recurring problem and/or that a replacement was already sent for the same cracked-base defect, rather than treating it as a first-time report or asking the customer to re-explain the original issue from scratch. A brief, empathetic acknowledgement is enough. The reply must NOT pretend there is no prior history and must NOT lecture the customer.
